pattern: Domestic Catalyst-Anchored Wins vs. Narrative-Driven Losses
description: Every winning position (WSM, EIX, PNC, JPM, OTIS) had an identifiable, dated domestic catalyst — earnings momentum, rate case events, or confirmed NII expansion — tied to the entry rationale. Every losing position was entered on a macro narrative, sector sentiment, or thematic story without a near-term binary event to anchor the trade. The absence of a concrete catalyst correlates perfectly with losses across both Core and Swing portfolios.
recommendation: Mandate a written catalyst checklist at entry: confirm a specific dated event (earnings, rate decision, contract award, product launch) occurring within the hold window before any position is opened, Core or Swing.

pattern: China Exposure Clustering Created Correlated Drawdowns
description: Four losing positions (TCOM, CIHKY, IDCBY, PDD) were all China-linked and all lost simultaneously, indicating the portfolio carried dangerous single-country concentration risk that was not recognized at construction. Each position was rationalized independently under different sub-narratives (travel recovery, financial diversification, quasi-sovereign safety, consumer growth) but they all shared the same China macro risk factor. A single China risk-off event wiped out capital across multiple supposedly diversified holdings.
recommendation: Implement a hard portfolio rule: total China-linked exposure across all tickers and portfolios cannot exceed 15% of total capital at any time; use a country exposure heatmap before each new entry to enforce this limit.

pattern: Early Exit Discipline Preserved Capital on Swing Losses
description: META (exited day 4, -2.2%) and GOOGL (exited day 3, -2.58%) were both cut well before the standard 10-day window, limiting losses to roughly one-third of what the losing China and staffing positions suffered by holding to full term. The pattern confirms that discretionary or stop-triggered early exits are the single most effective loss-mitigation tool available within this strategy's framework. The contrast with 10-day holds losing 8-19% is stark and quantifiable.
recommendation: Set a hard mechanical stop-loss at 3-4% below entry price for every swing position at the moment of purchase, removing discretion from the exit decision and preventing the 8-19% loss range seen in full-hold losers.

pattern: OTC and Thin-Volume ADRs Are Structurally Incompatible with Core Classification
description: CIHKY and IDCBY were both OTC-listed Chinese ADRs placed in a Core portfolio despite having thin US trading volume, wide bid-ask spreads, delisting overhang, and near-zero retail demand — characteristics that are the opposite of what a core holding requires. Classifying these as core positions reflects a fundamental mismatch between the portfolio label and the security's liquidity and risk profile. The losses in these names were partially structural, not just directional, as exits were costly regardless of market direction.
recommendation: Define explicit eligibility criteria for Core portfolio inclusion: minimum average daily US volume of $10M, primary US or EU listing, and no OTC classification; any security failing these screens is automatically excluded from Core consideration regardless of thesis quality.

pattern: High-Multiple Growth Stocks Without Near-Term Catalysts Underperform in Risk-Off Tape
description: NET, CRM, and GOOGL were all entered on AI or platform momentum themes without confirming that the broader tape was in a risk-on regime or that a near-term earnings event would compress the time-to-catalyst window. High revenue multiples in NET and CRM made both stocks disproportionately sensitive to any risk-off signal or rate expectation shift, amplifying losses beyond what the underlying business deterioration warranted. The multi-quarter nature of AI integration themes is fundamentally incompatible with 10-day swing trade holding periods.
recommendation: Before entering any stock trading above 15x revenue or 40x forward earnings in a swing position, require both a VIX below 20 confirming risk-on conditions AND an earnings date or product announcement within 10 calendar days of entry; if both conditions are not met, do not enter.

pattern: Pure-Play Sector Expression Outperforms Diversified Mega-Cap in Same Thesis
description: PNC (+4.43%) significantly outperformed JPM (+1.64%) despite both being entered on the same NII expansion thesis, demonstrating that regional banks — as higher-beta pure-plays on the rate environment — capture far more of the sector upside than diversified mega-cap banks that dilute the thesis across multiple revenue streams. This pattern suggests that when a sector thesis is high-conviction, capital should be concentrated in the most direct expression of that thesis rather than spread across large-cap proxies for safety. The size and diversification of JPM actually reduced return without meaningfully reducing risk.
recommendation: When a sector thesis drives a trade, rank candidate tickers by revenue concentration in the target theme and allocate the largest position weight to the highest-purity expression; reserve mega-cap names only for hedging or volatility-dampening roles with correspondingly smaller sizing.
